what: Clean, Parse, Harmonize, Match, and Geocode Messy Real-World US Addresses
title: Clean, Parse, Harmonize, Match, and Geocode Messy Real-World US Addresses
text: Addresses validated collection often inconsistently formatted contain typographical phonetic noise, making difficult compare link address data. goal addr clean, parse, standardize, match, geocode messy, real-world US addresses R. addr uses included usaddress library tag address components build vctrs-based address vectors, including addr() vectors addr_number(), addr_street(), addr_place() component vectors. vectors can standardized, matched, joined, used data-frame columns, allowing standard R tools work nested address structures.

Matching staged: ZIP codes matched first, streets matched within matched ZIP code, address numbers matched within matched ZIP/street group. keeps matching fast still allowing common street-name, phonetic, ZIP-code, address-number variation. title: Read National Address Database (NAD) tables into R — nad
text: U.S. Department Transportation partners address programs state, local, tribal governments compile authoritative data database. Find information : https://www.transportation.gov/gis/national-address-database nad_read() reads data NAD geodatabase county, downloading first R user's data directory addr package already downloaded nad_download(), readies R. Counties can identified either county name plus state, 5-digit county FIPS identifier. County names state abbreviations resolved internally still determine cache path source query. title: Create ZIP code variants — zipcode_variant
text: input ZIP code used generate variants (, e.g., 45220): minus1: subtracting one zipcode (45219) plus1: adding one zipcode (45221) sub5: substituting fifth digit ZIP code (45221, 45222, 45223, 45224, 45225, 45226, 45227, 45228, 45229) sub4: substituting fourth digit ZIP code (45200, 45210, 45230, 45240, 45250, 45260, 45270, 45280, 45290) swap: swapping second third digits ZIP code (42520) one variant type can created variants returned order requested (see examples).

title: Create ZIP code variants — zipcode_variant
code: zipcode_variant(x, variant = c("minus1", "plus1", "sub5", "sub4", "swap"))
